hero.headline: DPU-COL Online MBA in Digital Marketing Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: NAAC A++ | NIRF #41 University 2025 | UGC-DEB Approved | Total fees ₹1,89,400

sections.tldr: DPU-COL Online MBA in Digital Marketing Management covers introduction to digital marketing, SEO, integrated marketing communication, social media marketing, emerging trends in IT, web designing and content management, product and brand management, and international marketing. NAAC A++ NIRF #41. Total fee ₹1,89,400 with ₹10,000 discount. Zero-cost EMI from ₹2,500/month. edX certifications bundled. UGC-DEB approved.

about.body:
DPU-COL's MBA in Digital Marketing Management covers both the technical execution layer (SEO, SEM, social media marketing, content management, web design) and the strategic layer (integrated marketing communication, product and brand management, international marketing). The programme bridges the gap between digital marketing certifications (which teach platform skills) and traditional marketing MBAs (which lack digital depth).

Semester 3 covers the full digital marketing execution stack: SEO and SEM fundamentals, social media strategy across platforms, content and email marketing workflows, web design and content management systems, and emerging IT trends affecting digital channels. Semester 4 adds the strategic and international dimension: product and brand management in digital-first markets, international marketing with digital channel focus, plus project work.

The Generic Elective options (Digital Marketing or Business English in Sem 3; Soft Skills or Business Ethics and Corporate Governance in Sem 4) allow students to deepen either the technical or the professional development track.

Dr. D.Y. Patil Vidyapeeth, Pune holds NAAC A++ and NIRF #41 (University 2025). edX certifications are bundled. At ₹1,89,400 with zero-cost EMI from ₹2,500/month, the programme is mid-range for a NAAC A++ digital marketing MBA.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA management core. Semesters 3-4 below show the Digital Marketing Management specialisation subjects.

subjects.name: Introduction to Digital Marketing
subjects.desc: Digital marketing ecosystem overview, digital vs traditional marketing ROI comparison, customer journey mapping across digital touchpoints, and digital marketing strategy frameworks.

subjects.name: Search Engine Optimization
subjects.desc: On-page and off-page SEO, keyword research methodology, technical SEO (site speed, schema markup, crawlability), Google Search Console analytics, and SEO audit frameworks.

subjects.name: Integrated Marketing Communication
subjects.desc: IMC planning frameworks, multi-channel campaign coordination, ATL-BTL integration with digital channels, brand consistency across platforms, and media mix optimisation.

subjects.name: Social Media Marketing
subjects.desc: Platform strategy (Instagram, LinkedIn, X, YouTube), content calendar design, community management, influencer marketing frameworks, and social media analytics and reporting.

subjects.name: Emerging Trends in Information Technology
subjects.desc: AI in marketing automation, programmatic advertising, AR/VR in customer engagement, voice search optimisation, and MarTech stack evolution.

subjects.name: Web Designing and Content Management
subjects.desc: Website UX principles, CMS platforms (WordPress overview), landing page optimisation, mobile-first design, and content governance frameworks.

subjects.name: Generic Elective-I: Digital Marketing or Business English
subjects.desc: Students select one elective. Digital Marketing deepens campaign execution skills. Business English strengthens professional communication for marketing leadership.

subjects.name: Product and Brand Management
subjects.desc: Brand identity design, brand equity measurement, product lifecycle management in digital markets, new product launch strategy, and brand positioning frameworks.

subjects.name: International Marketing
subjects.desc: Global market entry strategies for digital products, cross-border e-commerce, international digital advertising regulations (GDPR, data localisation), and cultural adaptation for global campaigns.

subjects.name: Generic Elective-II: Soft Skills or Business Ethics and Corporate Governance
subjects.desc: Students select one elective. Soft Skills covers presentation, negotiation, and team leadership. Business Ethics covers corporate governance, CSR, and ethical marketing practices.

subjects.name: Project Work Report
subjects.desc: Industry-linked digital marketing project. Typically a campaign strategy, SEO audit, social media analytics study, or brand positioning project. Assessed by faculty and external evaluator.

whoHires.body:
DPU-COL MBA Digital Marketing Management graduates target roles across digital agencies, in-house marketing teams, and e-commerce:

**Industries:** Digital marketing agencies, e-commerce, FMCG (digital marketing divisions), banking (digital acquisition), media and entertainment, edtech, healthtech, and SaaS companies

**Entry-level roles:** Digital Marketing Executive, SEO Analyst, Social Media Executive, Content Marketing Coordinator, Performance Marketing Analyst, Email Marketing Specialist

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Digital Marketing Manager, SEO Manager, Social Media Manager, Performance Marketing Manager, Brand Manager (digital), Content Strategy Lead

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Head of Digital Marketing, VP Marketing (digital-first companies), CMO (D2C and e-commerce), Director of Growth, Head of Performance Marketing

**Employers:** Digital agencies (Dentsu, GroupM, WPP agencies), e-commerce (Flipkart, Amazon, Myntra marketing), FMCG digital (HUL, P&G, ITC digital divisions), D2C brands (Mamaearth, BoAt, Sugar Cosmetics), and IT services (digital marketing practice leads).

**Note:** DPU-COL provides placement assistance for all MBA programmes.

skills.body:
The Digital Marketing Management specialisation builds both execution and strategic marketing skills:

**Technical skills:**
- SEO: keyword research, on-page optimisation, technical audit, backlink analysis, Google Search Console and Analytics interpretation
- Social Media: platform-specific content strategy, paid social campaign setup, community management protocols, influencer outreach and contract basics
- Performance Marketing: Google Ads campaign structure, Meta Ads Manager basics, attribution modelling, and ROAS calculation
- Web and Content: CMS management, landing page A/B testing, content calendar planning, and email marketing automation setup

**Strategic skills:**
- Brand Management: brand equity frameworks (Keller's CBBE model), positioning strategy, brand architecture for multi-product companies
- IMC Planning: media mix modelling, budget allocation across digital channels, and campaign measurement frameworks
- International Digital Marketing: cross-border advertising compliance, multi-language content strategy, and global platform differences

**Soft skills:** Presenting digital campaign ROI to C-suite, managing creative and technical teams, negotiating with media vendors and influencers, and leading digital transformation initiatives in traditional marketing organisations.

comparisons.body:
Comparing DPU-COL Online MBA Digital Marketing Management against peer programmes:

**vs Chandigarh University Online MBA Digital Marketing:** CU at ₹1,65,000 with NIRF #19 and PwC co-teaching covers digital marketing as one of 26 specialisations. CU wins on NIRF rank and fee. DPU at ₹1,89,400 holds NAAC A++ (vs CU A+) and DPU's curriculum includes web design, content management, and emerging IT trends as dedicated subjects alongside SEO and social media. For NIRF-sensitive employers, CU is stronger. For comprehensive digital marketing curriculum depth, DPU competes.

**vs LPU Online MBA Digital Marketing:** LPU at approximately ₹2,00,000 includes Coursera Plus and placement assistance. DPU at ₹1,89,400 saves ₹10,600 with stronger NAAC (A++ vs A+) and NIRF ranking. DPU includes edX certifications vs LPU's Coursera Plus.

**vs Shoolini Online MBA Digital Marketing:** Shoolini at ₹1,30,000-₹1,50,000 with NIRF #69 covers digital marketing at a lower fee point. Shoolini wins on fee. DPU wins on NIRF rank (#41 vs #69) and NAAC grade (A++ vs A+).

**vs Amrita Online MBA Marketing:** Amrita at ₹2,20,000 with NIRF #8 covers marketing broadly but not digital marketing as a standalone specialisation. For candidates specifically targeting digital marketing management roles, DPU's dedicated spec is more directly relevant.

fees.body:
The DPU-COL Online MBA in Digital Marketing Management has a total programme fee of ₹1,89,400 for Indian students (USD 3,600 for international students).

**Semester-wise fee breakdown:**
- Semester 1: ₹50,000
- Semester 2: ₹50,000
- Semester 3: ₹45,000
- Semester 4: ₹44,400
- **Total: ₹1,89,400**

**Scholarship:** ₹10,000 off for full payment in one or two transactions within 15 days (effective fee ₹1,79,400).

**Payment options:**
- Zero-cost EMI: from ₹2,500/month directly through DPU-COL (no external NBFC, no interest)
- Semester-wise payments as above
- No application fee

**Bundled:** edX certifications included at no extra cost.

Fees are indicative. Reconfirm at dypatilonline.com before payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Digital Marketing Management as the specialisation, issued by Dr. D.Y. Patil Vidyapeeth, Pune (DPU-COL) under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A++ accreditation and NIRF #41 University rank. It is valid for government and private sector recruitment. edX certifications supplement the MBA credential. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from DPU-COL Online MBA Digital Marketing Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Pooja L.
items.liked: SEO and Social Media Marketing subjects were practical — I applied the audit framework directly to my agency's client accounts
items.disliked: Emerging Trends in IT felt too broad — would have preferred deeper coverage of marketing automation platforms (HubSpot, Marketo) specifically
items.body: I work as a digital marketing manager at a mid-size agency. The SEO, social media, and IMC subjects gave me the structured frameworks I was applying intuitively. NAAC A++ and NIRF #41 gave the MBA credibility when I pitched for senior client accounts where credentials matter. edX certifications added practical depth.

items.name: Aditya R.
items.liked: Product and Brand Management module helped me transition from execution-only digital marketing to brand strategy thinking
items.disliked: Performance marketing (Google Ads, Meta Ads) could have been covered as a dedicated subject rather than spread across multiple modules
items.body: I transitioned from a performance marketing executive to a brand manager role at a D2C company. The combination of digital execution subjects (Sem 3) and brand strategy (Sem 4) gave me the full-stack marketing vocabulary. DPU's zero-cost EMI from ₹2,500/month was manageable on my pre-transition salary.

items.name: Nisha T.
items.liked: Web Designing and Content Management gave me enough CMS and UX understanding to lead website redesign projects without depending entirely on developers
items.disliked: International Marketing content was too traditional — more focus on cross-border D2C, Shopify-based global e-commerce, and international payment gateways would modernise this module
items.body: I head content marketing at an edtech company. The content management, SEO, and brand management subjects were directly applicable. Having an MBA from a NIRF #41 NAAC A++ university gave me the credential weight that digital marketing certifications alone could not provide during my promotion cycle.

faqs.question: Is this MBA only about digital marketing execution or does it cover marketing strategy?
faqs.answer: It covers both. Semester 3 focuses on digital marketing execution: SEO, social media, content management, and emerging digital trends. Semester 4 adds the strategic layer: product and brand management, international marketing, and integrated marketing communication. The management core in Semesters 1-2 covers finance, strategy, and organisational behaviour. Graduates are positioned for both execution leadership and marketing strategy roles.

faqs.question: How does DPU Digital Marketing Management compare to CU's Digital Marketing MBA?
faqs.answer: CU at ₹1,65,000 with NIRF #19 wins on rank and fee. DPU at ₹1,89,400 holds NAAC A++ (vs CU A+) and includes dedicated subjects on web design, content management, and emerging IT trends that CU's digital marketing spec may not cover as standalone courses. Both are UGC-DEB approved.

faqs.question: Does the programme teach Google Ads and Meta Ads?
faqs.answer: Performance marketing concepts are covered across the SEO, social media marketing, and digital marketing introduction subjects. Google Ads and Meta Ads campaign setup is covered at a management level (campaign structure, budget allocation, ROAS measurement) rather than at a platform-certification level. Candidates needing deep platform-specific skills should supplement with Google Ads certification or Meta Blueprint.

faqs.question: What is the total fee?
faqs.answer: ₹1,89,400 total (Sem 1: ₹50,000, Sem 2: ₹50,000, Sem 3: ₹45,000, Sem 4: ₹44,400). ₹10,000 scholarship for upfront payment within 15 days. Zero-cost EMI from ₹2,500/month. No application fee. edX certifications bundled. Reconfirm at dypatilonline.com.

faqs.question: What electives are available?
faqs.answer: Semester 3 offers Generic Elective-I: choose between Digital Marketing (deeper campaign execution) or Business English (professional communication). Semester 4 offers Generic Elective-II: choose between Soft Skills (leadership, negotiation) or Business Ethics and Corporate Governance. These are selected by the student at the start of each semester.

chooseIf: You want a dedicated Digital Marketing Management MBA from a NAAC A++ NIRF #41 university with edX certifications.
Your target is digital marketing leadership — agency head, brand manager, head of digital — where an MBA credential adds weight beyond certifications.
₹1,89,400 with zero-cost EMI is within budget and NAAC A++ meets your employer's credential filter.
You need both execution skills (SEO, social, content) and strategy (brand management, IMC, international marketing) in one programme.
consider: NIRF top 20 is your employer's hard filter — CU (NIRF #19) at ₹1,65,000 is the stronger choice.
You only need digital marketing execution skills without the full MBA — a Google Digital Marketing certificate or platform-specific certification is faster and cheaper.
You want broader marketing management (not digital-specific) — consider DPU's Marketing Management or Amrita's Marketing spec.
